title: Bridge Essay: Individual Differences in Bodily Self-Consciousness and Its Neural Basis.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Birth/Body** (Phase 1) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Birth/Body to mechanistic neuroscience through Individual, Differences, in, Bodily, Self-Consciousness.

**Individual Differences in Bodily Self-Consciousness and Its Neural Basis.** — Brain Sci. PMID: 39199487. DOI: 10.3390/brainsci14080795. Bodily self-consciousness (BSC), asubject of interdisciplinary interest, refers to the awareness of one'sbodily states. Previous studies have noted the existence of individual differences in BSC, while neglecting the underlying factors and neural basis of such individual differences. Considering that BSC relied on integration from both internal and external self-relevant information, we here review previous findings on individual differences in BSC through athree-level-self model, which inclu
